input.prompt:
I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and

input.initialPrompt:
I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

data.content:
Here's a CollectionView with single selection and pull-to-refresh:

**XAML:**
```xml
<RefreshView Command="{Binding RefreshCommand}"
             IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}">
    <CollectionView ItemsSource="{Binding Items}"
                    SelectionMode="Single"
                    SelectedItem="{Binding SelectedItem}"
                    SelectionChangedCommand="{Binding SelectionChangedCommand}">
        <CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
            <DataTemplate x:DataType="models:Item">
                <Grid Padding="10">
                    <Label Text="{Binding Name}" FontSize="16" />
                </Grid>
            </DataTemplate>
        </CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
    </CollectionView>
</RefreshView>
```

**ViewModel bindings needed:**

| Property/Command | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `Items` (ObservableCollection) | Data source |
| `SelectedItem` | Bound two-way; set to `null` after navigation to deselect |
| `SelectionChangedCommand` | Triggers navigation to detail page |
| `RefreshCommand` (async) | Calls your API to reload data |
| `IsRefreshing` (bool) | Set `false` when refresh completes |

**Key ViewModel logic:**

```csharp
[RelayCommand]
async Task OnSelectionChanged()
{
    if (SelectedItem is null) return;
    await Shell.Current.GoToAsync(nameof(DetailPage), new Dictionary<string, object>
    {
        { "Item", SelectedItem }
    });
    SelectedItem = null; // reset so user can re-select same item
}

[RelayCommand]
async Task Refresh()
{
    Items = new ObservableCollection<Item>(await _apiService.GetItemsAsync());
    IsRefreshing = false;
}
```

**Notes:**
- `SelectionMode="Single"` ensures only one item is selected at a time.
- Nulling `SelectedItem` after navigation allows re-selecting the same row later.
- `RefreshView` wraps the entire `CollectionView`; `IsRefreshing` must be set to `false` explicitly or the spinner won't dismiss.
data.reasoningText: The user is asking for a .NET MAUI (or Xamarin.Forms) CollectionView implementation with single item selection and pull-to-refresh, along with XAML and ViewModel binding explanations. This is a general programming question that doesn't require any tool usage - I can answer it directly from my knowledge.
